x-non-json-responses: Known non-JSON responses not documented in this spec: 302 redirects from @no_authenticated_users_allowed and @email_validation_required auth decorators, and HTML 404s from abort()/get_or_404().

delete.description: Remove a member from a UTub

post.description: Add a member to a UTub
Ingest a batch of UI-category metrics events from the browser

parameters.name: transport
parameters.description: Optional transport hint set by the metrics-client's `flushBeacon()` unload path; `flush()` omits the param. Reserved for future pipeline-health telemetry to distinguish fetch-vs-beacon transport volume in the `API_METRICS_INGEST_BATCH` event.

200.description:
Response payload returned by `POST /api/metrics`.

    `accepted` is the number of events that the route handed off to
    `record_event(...)`. Because the writer is log-and-drop, individual events
    may have been silently dropped at the Redis layer; the field is informative
    but not authoritative.

get.description: Return the top events by total count for an admin time window.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Relative time window: day | week | month | year | Nh | Nd. Validated by parse_window() at the route layer. Mutually exclusive with `start`+`end`.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Inclusive start of an absolute range (ISO-8601 with timezone — e.g., `2026-06-06T00:00:00Z` or `2026-06-06T00:00:00+05:00`). Naive datetimes are rejected at the schema layer via `AwareDatetime`. Must be paired with `end` and is mutually exclusive with `window`.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Exclusive end of an absolute range (ISO-8601 with timezone — same format as `start`). Must be paired with `start` and is mutually exclusive with `window`.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Optional category filter (api | domain | ui).

parameters.name: resource
parameters.description: Optional resource filter (utub | url | tag | member | auth | search | form | deck | nav | error | contact | admin | other). Requires `category`; the resource must appear in `RESOURCE_BY_CATEGORY[category]`.

parameters.name: device_type
parameters.description: Optional device-type filter (1=mobile, 2=desktop).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of rows to return (1-100).

get.description: Return per-bucket counts for a single event over an admin time window.

parameters.name: event_name
parameters.description: Any EventName value (api, domain, or ui).

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Relative time window: day | week | month | year | Nh | Nd. Validated by parse_window() at the route layer. Mutually exclusive with `start`+`end`.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Inclusive start of an absolute range (ISO-8601 with timezone — e.g., `2026-06-06T00:00:00Z` or `2026-06-06T00:00:00+05:00`). Naive datetimes are rejected at the schema layer via `AwareDatetime`. Must be paired with `end` and is mutually exclusive with `window`.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Exclusive end of an absolute range (ISO-8601 with timezone — same format as `start`). Must be paired with `start` and is mutually exclusive with `window`.

parameters.name: resolution
parameters.description: date_trunc resolution: hour (default) or day.

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: Optional Flask endpoint name (e.g. utubs.get_single_utub). When supplied alongside event_name=api_hit, filters the series to only rows matching this endpoint — used by the admin dashboard's API tab to chart per-endpoint timeseries.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: Optional HTTP method (GET, POST, etc.). When supplied alongside event_name=api_hit, narrows the series to one (endpoint, method) pair so two methods on the same endpoint stay separate.

parameters.name: device_type
parameters.description: Optional device-type filter (1=mobile, 2=desktop).

get.description: Return per-category totals for the current and immediately-preceding window.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Relative time window: day | week | month | year | Nh | Nd. Validated by parse_window() at the route layer. Mutually exclusive with `start`+`end`.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Inclusive start of an absolute range (ISO-8601 with timezone — e.g., `2026-06-06T00:00:00Z` or `2026-06-06T00:00:00+05:00`). Naive datetimes are rejected at the schema layer via `AwareDatetime`. Must be paired with `end` and is mutually exclusive with `window`.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Exclusive end of an absolute range (ISO-8601 with timezone — same format as `start`). Must be paired with `start` and is mutually exclusive with `window`.

get.description: Return per-(bucket × dim tuple) counts for a single event over an admin time window, grouped by 1-3 JSONB dimension keys.

parameters.name: event_name
parameters.description: Any EventName value (api, domain, or ui).

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description: List of dimension field names to group the timeseries by. Each entry must be a field of `DIMENSION_MODELS[event_name]` (validated at the route layer); shape is bounded at the schema layer to 1-3 non-empty entries ≤64 chars each.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Relative time window: day | week | month | year | Nh | Nd. Validated by parse_window() at the route layer. Mutually exclusive with `start`+`end`.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Inclusive start of an absolute range (ISO-8601 with timezone — e.g., `2026-06-06T00:00:00Z` or `2026-06-06T00:00:00+05:00`). Naive datetimes are rejected at the schema layer via `AwareDatetime`. Must be paired with `end` and is mutually exclusive with `window`.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Exclusive end of an absolute range (ISO-8601 with timezone — same format as `start`). Must be paired with `start` and is mutually exclusive with `window`.

parameters.name: resolution
parameters.description: date_trunc resolution: hour (default) or day.

200.description:
Envelope returned by `GET /api/metrics/query/grouped-timeseries`.

    Unlike the single-series `timeseries` endpoint, the grouped variant does
    NOT zero-fill empty `(bucket, dim-tuple)` combinations. Cross-product
    expansion is expensive; the frontend renderer treats absent combos as
    "no segment for that bucket".

get.description: Assemble one conversion funnel (UI intent → API request → domain outcome) for an admin time window, fanning out per-step counts plus per-cause drop-off breakdowns from the server-side FLOWS registry.

parameters.name: flow_id
parameters.description: Which funnel to assemble (create_utub | add_url_to_utub | register | login).

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Relative time window: day | week | month | year | Nh | Nd. Validated by parse_window() at the route layer. Mutually exclusive with `start`+`end`.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Inclusive start of an absolute range (ISO-8601 with timezone — e.g., `2026-06-06T00:00:00Z` or `2026-06-06T00:00:00+05:00`). Naive datetimes are rejected at the schema layer via `AwareDatetime`. Must be paired with `end` and is mutually exclusive with `window`.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Exclusive end of an absolute range (ISO-8601 with timezone — same format as `start`). Must be paired with `start` and is mutually exclusive with `window`.

200.description:
Envelope returned by `GET /api/metrics/query/flow`.

    A `steps` wrapper (not a bare list) is required because
    `APIResponse.to_response()` spreads the payload via `**data_dict`, which
    needs a `BaseModel`-derived schema; the wire payload is `{"steps": [...]}`.

get.description: Return every gauge's windowed sample series for an admin time window in one batched response, each series folding in its kind + description.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: Relative time window: day | week | month | year | Nh | Nd. Validated by parse_window() at the route layer. Mutually exclusive with `start`+`end`.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Inclusive start of an absolute range (ISO-8601 with timezone — e.g., `2026-06-06T00:00:00Z` or `2026-06-06T00:00:00+05:00`). Naive datetimes are rejected at the schema layer via `AwareDatetime`. Must be paired with `end` and is mutually exclusive with `window`.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: Exclusive end of an absolute range (ISO-8601 with timezone — same format as `start`). Must be paired with `start` and is mutually exclusive with `window`.

200.description:
Batched envelope returned by `GET /api/metrics/query/gauges/timeseries`.

    Carries every gauge's windowed series in one response (mirrors
    `GroupedTimeseriesResponseSchema`'s envelope shape). A gauge with no rows in
    the window is absent from `gauges` rather than zero-filled.

status.description: Response status: Success, Failure, or No change

subject.description: Subject line of the contact message

content.description: Body content of the contact message

status.description: Response status: Success, Failure, or No change

errors.description: Map of field names to lists of validation error messages, if applicable

urlString.description: URL string involved in the error, if applicable

utubID.description: ID of the UTub the member was added to or removed from

member.description: User object for the member added or removed

MetricsIngestEvent.description:
One UI metrics event submitted by the browser.

Per-event dimension validation lives at the route layer
(`validate_dimensions(...)`) — not in a `@model_validator` here. Pydantic
v2 wraps `ValueError`/`AssertionError` raised inside validators, but a
`ValidationError` raised inside a validator does NOT round-trip cleanly
through that wrapper, so dimension shape checks happen post-decoration.

event_name.description: UI-category EventName value emitted by the browser

dimensions.description: Per-event dimension dict; shape enforced server-side via the matching `_Dim<EventName>` Pydantic model. Auto-injected with `device_type` by the metrics-client for all UI events, so the dict is always non-empty when sent from the browser.

events.description: Batch of UI-category metrics events to ingest (1-100 entries)

batch_id.description: Client-generated batch identifier; used server-side for SET NX EX idempotency on retries

MetricsIngestResponseSchema.description:
Response payload returned by `POST /api/metrics`.

`accepted` is the number of events that the route handed off to
`record_event(...)`. Because the writer is log-and-drop, individual events
may have been silently dropped at the Redis layer; the field is informative
but not authoritative.

accepted.description: Count of events accepted by the metrics ingest endpoint

TopEventRow.description: One row of the `top` query response — a single event aggregated over the window.

description.description: Human-readable event description. For UI/domain rows this comes from EventRegistry; for API rows it comes from the route's `@api_route(description=...)` kwarg.

api_endpoint.description: Raw Flask endpoint name (e.g. `utubs.get_single_utub`) — populated only for API-category rows so the dashboard can filter the timeseries chart by the exact column value stored in `AnonymousMetrics.endpoint`. Null for UI/domain rows.

total_count.description: Sum of counts across all buckets in the window

previous_count.description: Sum of counts for the same event across the immediately-preceding window of equal length. Zero when the event did not appear in the previous window. Used by the admin dashboard to render per-event delta-vs-prev arrows.

window.description: Window value as supplied by the client; null when the client supplied an absolute `start`/`end` range instead.

window_start.description: Inclusive UTC start of the window

window_end.description: Exclusive UTC end of the window

category.description: EventCategory filter applied to the query, or null if none

resource.description: Resource filter applied to the query (utub | url | tag | member | auth | search | form | deck | nav | error | contact | admin | other), or null if none. Requires `category` to also be set.

events.description: Top-N rows ordered by total_count descending

TimeseriesBucketSchema.description: One bucket of the `timeseries` query response.

count.description: Sum of counts within this bucket

event_name.description: EventName the series is filtered to

window.description: Window value as supplied by the client; null when the client supplied an absolute `start`/`end` range instead.

window_start.description: Inclusive UTC start of the window

window_end.description: Exclusive UTC end of the window

SummaryCategoryCount.description:
Per-category current/previous totals for the `summary` query response.

Returned as a list (not a dict) because `APIResponse` spreads payloads into
the top-level JSON body — a dict-of-category-to-int would collide with the
envelope's reserved keys.

current.description: Sum of counts in the current window

previous.description: Sum of counts in the immediately-preceding window

window.description: Window value as supplied by the client; null when the client supplied an absolute `start`/`end` range instead.

window_start.description: Inclusive UTC start of the window

window_end.description: Exclusive UTC end of the window

previous_window_start.description: Inclusive UTC start of the immediately-preceding window

previous_window_end.description: Exclusive UTC end of the immediately-preceding window

last_flush_at.description: Flush worker's liveness sentinel — UTC timestamp parsed from the `metrics:flush:last_success_epoch` Redis key, which the worker stamps on every successful run (including empty flushes). Reflects worker cadence, NOT data freshness — advances every minute regardless of traffic. Null when metrics are disabled, the sentinel is absent, or Redis is unreachable.

last_event_at.description: Wall-clock timestamp of the most recent AnonymousMetrics bucket (`MAX(bucketStart)`); null when the table is empty. Reflects when the last event was bucketed — advances only when traffic lands. Surfaced separately from `last_flush_at` so an admin can distinguish 'worker is dead' from 'nobody is using the app'.

GroupedTimeseriesBucket.description: One row of the `grouped-timeseries` response — a single bucket × dim-tuple.

dimensions.description: The dimension-tuple values for this row, keyed by the requested `group_by` fields. Closed-set values come from the per-event Literal dim model; typed loosely (`str | int`) for transport.

count.description: Sum of counts within this (bucket, dim-tuple)

GroupedTimeseriesResponseSchema.description:
Envelope returned by `GET /api/metrics/query/grouped-timeseries`.

Unlike the single-series `timeseries` endpoint, the grouped variant does
NOT zero-fill empty `(bucket, dim-tuple)` combinations. Cross-product
expansion is expensive; the frontend renderer treats absent combos as
"no segment for that bucket".

event_name.description: EventName the series is filtered to

window.description: Window value as supplied by the client; null when the client supplied an absolute `start`/`end` range instead.

window_start.description: Inclusive UTC start of the window

window_end.description: Exclusive UTC end of the window

group_by.description: Dimension field names the series is grouped by

buckets.description: Per-(bucket × dim tuple) rows, ordered chronologically and then alphabetically by dim values for deterministic output. NOT zero-filled — missing combinations are absent rather than zero-valued.

FlowBreakdownRow.description:
One per-cause row of a flow step's drop-off breakdown.

`pct_of_step` normalizes WITHIN the breakdown (all rows sum to ~1.0) so
cause-pill widths are self-consistent regardless of the step's absolute
count. It is a `float` (never null): a breakdown with no rows collapses to
`null` on the owning step (DD-6) before any row is constructed.

label.description: Raw dimension value for this cause (e.g. 'escape_key', 'invalid_url'); the renderer maps it to a human-readable label.

count.description: Summed count for this cause in the window.

pct_of_step.description: Fraction of the breakdown total this cause represents (0.0-1.0); all rows in one breakdown sum to ~1.0.

FlowStepSchema.description: One step of an assembled funnel returned by `GET /api/metrics/query/flow`.

event_name.description: The underlying event name counted for this step; for API steps this carries the step's display label instead (the Flask endpoint name is an internal routing identifier, not display-suitable).

count.description: Summed count for this step in the window.

pct_of_top.description: This step's count as a fraction of the funnel-top (steps[0]) count, capped at 1.0. Null when the top count is zero (division-by-zero guard, DD-6 graceful-degrade).

breakdown.description: Per-cause drop-off rows for the transition INTO this step; null when the step has no configured breakdown or the breakdown event has no rows in the window (DD-6 graceful-degrade).

FlowResponseSchema.description:
Envelope returned by `GET /api/metrics/query/flow`.

A `steps` wrapper (not a bare list) is required because
`APIResponse.to_response()` spreads the payload via `**data_dict`, which
needs a `BaseModel`-derived schema; the wire payload is `{"steps": [...]}`.

steps.description: Ordered funnel steps assembled server-side from the FLOWS registry; one entry per FlowDefinition step, in funnel order.

GaugeSampleSchema.description:
One sampled point of a gauge's timeseries.

COUNT/MAX gauges populate `value_int`; AVG gauges populate `value_float`
(the other stays null). A k-anon-suppressed `max_*` sample has BOTH null —
the renderer drops such points before charting.

sampled_at.description: UTC instant this gauge was sampled

value_int.description: Integer value for COUNT/MAX gauges; null otherwise

value_float.description: Fractional value for AVG gauges; null otherwise

GaugeSeries.description:
One gauge's full windowed series with its folded-in metadata.

`kind`/`description` are folded into each series so the batched response is
self-describing — the dashboard renders a card straight from the entry, with
all gauge data sourced from this single batched endpoint.

GaugesTimeseriesResponseSchema.description:
Batched envelope returned by `GET /api/metrics/query/gauges/timeseries`.

Carries every gauge's windowed series in one response (mirrors
`GroupedTimeseriesResponseSchema`'s envelope shape). A gauge with no rows in
the window is absent from `gauges` rather than zero-filled.

window.description: Window value as supplied by the client; null when the client supplied an absolute `start`/`end` range instead.

window_start.description: Inclusive UTC start of the window

window_end.description: Exclusive UTC end of the window

gauges.description: One series per gauge that has samples in the window

email.description: Email address for the new account

status.description: Response status: Success, Failure, or No change

redirectUrl.description: URL to redirect to after login

status.description: Response status: Success, Failure, or No change

email.description: Email address associated with the account

status.description: Response status: Success, Failure, or No change

confirmNewPassword.description: New password confirmation, must match new password

status.description: Response status: Success, Failure, or No change
Response status: Success, Failure, or No change

UrlCreatedItemSchema.description: URL item shape for creation responses (same fields as UtubUrlDeleteSchema).

utubUrlID.description: Unique ID of the URL within the UTub

utubID.description: ID of the UTub the URL was added to

addedByUserID.description: User ID of the user who added the URL

utubTagID.description: Unique tag ID within the UTub

utubUrlID.description: Unique ID of the URL within the UTub

urlTags.description: List of tags applied to this URL

urlString.description: New URL string to replace the existing one

utubID.description: ID of the UTub containing the URL

utubName.description: Name of the UTub containing the URL

URL.description: Detailed URL item with updated URL string

urlTitle.description: New display title for the URL

URL.description: Detailed URL item with updated title

utubUrlID.description: Unique ID of the URL within the UTub

utubID.description: ID of the UTub the URL was deleted from

tagCountsInUtub.description: Map of tag ID to new applied count after deletion

utubName.description: Name of the UTub to create

utubID.description: Unique ID of the newly created UTub

utubName.description: Name of the newly created UTub

utubDescription.description: Description of the newly created UTub, or null if not set

utubCreatorID.description: User ID of the UTub creator

id.description: Unique tag ID within the UTub

tagApplied.description: Number of URLs in the UTub this tag is applied to

utubUrlID.description: Unique ID of the URL within the UTub

utubUrlTagIDs.description: List of tag IDs applied to this URL

canDelete.description: Whether the current user can delete this URL

UtubDetailSchema.description: Full UTub detail with members, URLs, and tags

createdByUserID.description: User ID of the UTub creator

members.description: List of members in the UTub

urls.description: List of URLs in the UTub

tags.description: List of tags used in the UTub

isCreator.description: Whether the current user is the creator of the UTub

currentUser.description: ID of the currently authenticated user

memberRole.description: Role of the current user in the UTub

utubs.description: List of UTubs the user is a member of

utubID.description: ID of the UTub whose name was updated

utubDescription.description: New description for the UTub, or empty to clear

utubID.description: ID of the UTub whose description was updated

utubDescription.description: New description of the UTub, or null if cleared
Description of the deleted UTub, or null if not set

utubUrlTagIDs.description: Updated list of tag IDs applied to the URL

utubTag.description: Tag that was added or removed from the URL

tagCountsInUtub.description: New count of URLs this tag is applied to after modification

utubTag.description: Tag that was added to the UTub

tagCountsInUtub.description: New count of URLs this tag is applied to after addition

utubTag.description: Tag that was deleted from the UTub

utubUrlIDs.description: List of URL IDs that had this tag removed

adminSession.description: Admin authorization is enforced by a Flask-Login session-role check (User_Role.ADMIN), NOT a request header or bearer token. This scheme is declared only to mark routes that additionally require admin role on top of an authenticated session.
